name: INR/USD Exchange Rate
summary: ₹93.01, flat — caught between NOP cap mechanical support and deadline pressure. NOP cap forcing $30-40B dollar unwinding by April 10 — $11-15B still outstanding. Banks requesting extension, no RBI response. Saturday session April 11. If deadline expires: rupee likely back through 95. DXY 99.92.

price_snapshots.delta_context: Rupee was already under pressure from FII outflows in 2025

price_snapshots.trigger: Record low on Iraq force majeure + oil spike
price_snapshots.delta_context: Goldman forecast ₹95 if oil stays elevated
RBI close 95.09, intraday hit 94.85 on previous day. Goldman predicted 95 on March 18 — confirmed in 9 days. Down 9.3% from pre-war ~87.

signals.content: Rupee breached ₹92/USD for first time. RBI sold $18-20B in single week. Foreign exchange reserves declined $11.68B by Mar 7.
Rupee hit record ₹93.797/USD. Goldman Sachs predicted ₹95 amid oil surge. BusinessToday asked: 'Is the rupee headed for 100 mark?'

Rupee weakened to fresh record 93.89/USD in early trade despite Trump de-escalation. India's markets fell with Asia (traded before Trump announcement). RBI continues heavy intervention but reserves declining.

Rupee hit intraday low of 94.85/USD on March 27, closed at 94.81. Down 4%+ since war began. RBI intervening with net short dollar position approaching $100B. Forex reserves dropped $7B to $709.75B. Approaching psychological 95 level.

TRIGGER ACTIVATED: Rupee breached ₹95/USD — RBI reference rate closed at 95.09 on March 27 (confirmed by Business Standard, Bloomberg, The Week; yfinance shows 94.31 likely mid-session). Goldman's March 18 forecast of ₹95 confirmed in 9 days. RBI emergency response: imposed NOP-INR hard cap of $100 million on ALL authorized dealer banks regardless of balance sheet size (March 28), compliance deadline April 10. Previously banks could maintain NOP limits up to 25% of capital — this is explicitly anti-speculation and constitutes a form of capital flow management. Forex reserves dropped $30.14 billion in 3 weeks of March to $698.35 billion (below $700B). RBI's net short dollar forward position approaching $100 billion. The cascade: Brent $112→India imports 85-90%→import bill balloons→RBI burns reserves→reserves declining→market senses limits→FPI flight ($11.5B March)→rupee breaks 95→RBI imposes NOP cap→next steps are outright capital controls if this doesn't hold. (Sources: Business Standard, Bloomberg, The Week, RBI circular)

Rupee rallied to ₹93.88 (yfinance) on Monday Asian session — NOP cap forcing banks to sell dollars to close long-dollar onshore positions. MECHANICAL rally, not fundamental. Position size revised to $25-50B. RBI reference rate was ₹95.09 Friday; Monday RBI rate will be authoritative. Structural pressures (oil, FPI outflows, reserves) unchanged. (Sources: yfinance, Bloomberg, Business Standard)

EVENING UPDATE — NOP CAP FAILED WITHIN 4 HOURS: Rupee opened at 93.56 (125 paise stronger, banks forced to sell dollars by NOP cap). Gains evaporated within hours as oil surged from $107 to $116 on Trump 'take the oil' FT interview. Hit RECORD INTRADAY LOW of 95.22. Closed ~95.07. MUFG Research headlined: 'Can't stop this train.' Business Standard: 'Temporary relief for Rupee, not a trend reversal.' FXStreet: 'USD/INR recovers RBI-linked losses, reclaims all-time highs.' The RBI's escalation ladder (spot intervention → NOP cap) has been exhausted. Next steps: emergency rate hike or capital controls — both politically radioactive. (Sources: FXStreet, MUFG Research, Business Standard, Bloomberg, Freepressjournal)

CRITICAL DATA: yfinance shows ₹93.91 but RBI reference rate was ₹95.07 (March 30). Intraday low ₹95.22 (record). India markets closed March 31 (Mahavir Jayanti). The NOP cap → banks sold dollars → rupee opened at 93.56 → oil surged back to $115 on Trump FT interview → rupee collapsed to 95.22 within 4 hours. NOP cap failed. MUFG: 'Can't stop this train.' The gap: yfinance captures partial NOP-driven session print or offshore quote. Actual onshore rupee near record weakness at ₹95+. CONFIRMED.

Rupee trading volatile in 93.48-94.26 range (not at 95.22 record low). NOP cap deadline April 10 creating forced position unwinding by banks ($30-40B). RBI reference rate near 95 onshore vs yfinance 93.52 — divergence reflects offshore/onshore gap. India 10Y at 7.03% — higher yields should attract carry but FPI outflows dominating (₹1.14 lakh crore record March). DXY dropped below 100 (positive for INR) but India-specific structural pressures (oil imports, FPI selling, NOP unwinding) offsetting global dollar weakness. Jaishankar's 'dalal nation' remark signals government positioning away from mediation toward self-interest. (Sources: TradingEconomics, Business Standard, Bloomberg)

REPORTED: INR improved to 92.64 per TradingEconomics (April 2), recovering from 93.19 (yfinance April 1, -0.3% daily move = strengthening). Brent crash from $118 to $102 easing import bill pressure. NOP cap deadline April 10 creates mechanical INR buying pressure (banks unwinding $30-40B forex positions = positive for rupee short-term but bank profitability hit). Recovery is mechanical (NOP forced unwinding + oil crash), not fundamental — FPI outflows Rs 1.8 trillion FY26, structural pressures persist. (Sources: TradingEconomics, Business Standard)

Rupee in 92-94 range on April 2-3. NOP cap deadline April 10 — 7 days, no extension. $30-40B in forced unwinding creates mechanical pressure. RBI MPC April 6-8 — market expects hold at 5.25% but oil at $109 and inflation rising constrain rate cut options. FPI outflows ₹9,931 cr April 2 accelerating. India closed April 3 (Good Friday). (Sources: Business Standard, Bloomberg, TradingEconomics)

CONFIRMED: Rupee surged +1.8% to Rs 93.10/USD on April 4 — largest single-day gain since 2013. Driven by RBI NOP cap forcing banks into massive dollar liquidations ahead of April 10 deadline. This is REGULATORY FORCE, not organic improvement — underlying structural pressures (oil $109-112, FPI outflows Rs 1.8 trillion FY26) unchanged. yfinance shows 92.67 (offshore/NDF divergence). India markets CLOSED April 4-5. RBI MPC starts April 6, decision April 8 — expected hold at 5.25%. NOP cap deadline April 10 unchanged, $30-40B still unwinding. India 10Y at 7.11-7.13%. Monday April 7 gap risk: all weekend developments priced in one session. (Sources: PingTV India, ANI, Business Standard, Trading Economics, yfinance)

RBI MPC Day 1 underway (April 6-8, decision April 8). Rupee at 92.67 (offshore/yfinance). NOP cap deadline April 10 — 4 days away, $30-40B in forced unwinding continuing. Three sequential binaries this week: MPC rate decision (Apr 8), NOP compliance deadline (Apr 10), helium production cut window (Apr 7-10). India markets reopen Monday April 7 with all weekend war developments to price in one session. Structural pressures unchanged: oil at $120+, FPI outflows Rs 1.8 trillion FY26. (Sources: RBI, yfinance, Business Standard)

INR/USD stabilized at ₹93.04 (+0.1%), NOP cap forced liquidation dampening speculative pressure. Banks completing $30-40B dollar unwinding ahead of April 10 deadline — the unwinding itself is reducing uncertainty. India 10Y GOI eased to 7.05% (-5bps). DXY at 100.12 (above psychological 100 level). FPI outflows cumulative Rs 1.5 trillion in 2026 — structural pressure continues. Banks urging RBI to revisit NOP cap, flagging mark-to-market losses. Saturday trading session April 11 confirmed — provides additional compliance window. Oil at $111 = ongoing depreciation pressure on INR. (Sources: Yahoo Finance, Trading Economics, Business Standard, ANI)

Rupee at Rs 92.93/USD per script (slightly stronger than Bloomberg's earlier 95-100 warning bands). Holding 93 as NOP cap deadline approaches (April 10). Banks completing $30-40B dollar unwinding. Rupee vs Bloomberg 95/USD framing now baseline; Multibagg/Policy Circle noted ~95/USD per analysis. India 10Y GOI eased -5 bps to 7.05% on dovish RBI Day 1 read. FOR REFERENCE: 'Rupee crosses Rs 95/USD' trigger remains ACTIVE per April 6 brief. Live spot 92.93 is back BELOW 95 — but the trigger was activated based on intraday peaks earlier in the week. Triple binary T-72: deadline expires tonight; RBI MPC Apr 8; NOP cap Apr 10. If deadline expires, GIFT Nifty gaps -500 to -800 pts overnight; rupee likely back through 95 toward 100.

EVENING UPDATE: INR/USD at Rs 93.00-93.07 — flat, caught between NOP cap mechanical support and deadline pressure. $4,650 gold floor and DXY at 99.92 creating mixed signals. NOP cap deadline April 10 — $11-15B still unwinding. Banks requesting 3-month extension, no RBI response. Saturday trading April 11 provides additional compliance window. If deadline expires tonight, GIFT Nifty gaps -500 to -800 pts; rupee likely back through 95 toward 100. India 10Y at 7.05-7.10%. (Sources: MarketScreener, Business Standard, Trading Economics)

edges.context: Rupee at 92.67 offshore. NOP cap deadline April 10 forcing $30-40B unwinding. FPI outflows Rs 1.8 trillion FY26 — worst since 1992. Three sequential binaries this week.
Higher oil → more dollar demand for imports → rupee weakens. Oil at $120+ is the binding constraint. India imports 85-90% of crude.
NOP cap deadline April 10 firm — 4 days. $30-40B positions need squaring. RBI MPC Day 1 underway April 6-8. Decision April 8 — expected hold at 5.25%.
Rupee at 92.67 offshore. NOP cap unwinding creates mechanical INR buying. FPI outflows Rs 1.8 trillion FY26 — worst since 1992, 42% YoY increase.

trigger_points.mechanism: Would signal loss of RBI control. Emergency measures possible. FII exodus would accelerate dramatically.
